merger_name: AZ Next Generation Advisory – Intend Financial

acquirers.name: AZ NEXT GENERATION ADVISORY PTY LTD

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (**AZ Next Generation Advisory**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in BMM Partners Pty Ltd, trading as Intend Financial (**Intend Financial**).

AZ Next Generation Advisory is an Australian professional services advisory company established in 2014 that owns approximately 48 financial planning and accounting businesses with operations and customers across Australia, including in Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania (where Intend Financial has a presence). AZ Next Generation Advisory has approximately 404 licensed financial planners across its network.

Intend Financial is a financial planning business established in 2021 that provides financial planning services from four offices, in Victoria (Mornington and Hawthorn East), Queensland (Gold Coast) and Tasmania (Launceston), to customers across Australia. Intend Financial has 10 licensed financial planners.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (AZ Next Generation Advisory)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the share capital in BMM Partners Pty Ltd
(Intend Financial), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, AZ Next Generation Advisory, is an Australian
professional services advisory company established in 2014 that
owns approximately 48 financial planning and accounting
businesses with operations and customers across Australia,
including in Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania (where Intend
Financial has a presence). AZ Next Generation Advisory has
approximately 404 licensed financial planners across its network.
AZ Next Generation Advisory is a subsidiary of AZ Aust Holdings Pty
Ltd whose major shareholders are funds managed by Oaktree
Capital Management LP (Oaktree) and Azimut Holding S.p.a.
(Azimut). Oaktree is a global investment management firm and
Azimut is a global asset and wealth management business.
The target, Intend Financial, is a financial planning business
established in 2021 that provides financial planning services from
four offices, in Victoria (Mornington and Hawthorn East), Queensland
(Gold Coast) and Tasmania (Launceston), to customers across
Australia. Intend Financial has 10 licensed financial planners.
AZ Next Generation Advisory and Intend Financial both supply
financial planning services, which include wealth management,
retirement planning, insurance, investment, estate and succession,
debt management, business planning and superannuation advisory
services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between AZ Next
Generation Advisory and Intend Financial in the supply of
financial planning services in Northern Tasmania, Gold
Coast, Greater Melbourne and across Australia
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial
planning services in the relevant geographic areas is
estimated to be low and the increase in share of supply
arising from the Acquisition would be small
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of financial planning
services in Northern Tasmania, Gold Coast, Greater
Melbourne and across Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
